title: Resident Evil Requiem
subtitle: The ninth mainline entry in Capcom's survival horror franchise, Resident Evil Requiem continues the series' return to atmospheric horror with a new protagonist, a new setting, and a confirmed 2026 release window across PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC.
content_html:
Overview

Resident Evil Requiem (internally referred to as RE9) is the ninth numbered entry in Capcom's mainline Resident Evil franchise. Announced in late 2025 following a series of leaks and a brief teaser at The Game Awards, Requiem represents Capcom's next major chapter in survival horror after the critically acclaimed Resident Evil Village (2021) and its Winters' Expansion DLC.

The title continues Capcom's modern era approach, blending first-person exploration with third-person action sequences, dense atmosphere, and a tightly scripted narrative. Requiem is developed by Capcom Division 1, the internal studio responsible for Resident Evil 7, Resident Evil 2 Remake, Resident Evil 3 Remake, and Village. The game runs on the RE Engine, Capcom's proprietary engine that has powered every Resident Evil title since RE7 in 2017.

For all the latest Resident Evil Requiem news, reveals, and analysis, this hub page is updated with every confirmed detail. For broader gaming coverage see the OzoneNews Video Games hub.

Release Date | What Has Been Confirmed

Capcom has confirmed a 2026 release window for Resident Evil Requiem but has not announced a specific date as of June 2026. Industry analysts and leakers have pointed to a Q3 or Q4 2026 window based on Capcom's historical release cadence and the game's apparent production stage, which is believed to be in final certification testing based on insider reports.

A formal release date announcement is widely expected at a major gaming showcase in the summer of 2026. The game was conspicuously absent from Sony's June 2 State of Play, fueling speculation that a dedicated Capcom event or The Game Awards reveal is more likely for the final date confirmation.

Capcom has confirmed that Resident Evil Requiem will not be a timed exclusive on any platform and will launch simultaneously across all supported platforms.

Story and Setting | What We Know

Resident Evil Requiem is set in a dense urban environment, marking the franchise's most significant return to a city setting since Resident Evil 3 (1999) and the Raccoon City saga. The setting is a mid-sized European city that has been cut off from outside communication following an unspecified bioterrorism incident. Players navigate collapsing city infrastructure, underground tunnels, and abandoned civic buildings as the situation deteriorates across the game's timeline.

The protagonist is a new character, a female operative with ties to both the BSAA and a private security contractor whose history intersects with the incident. Capcom has not released the character's full name or backstory as of June 2026. Series veterans Chris Redfield and Leon S. Kennedy are rumored to appear, though neither has been confirmed in official materials.

Thematically, Requiem is described by Capcom as a return to the series' roots in isolation, dread, and resource scarcity after Village's more action-oriented second half drew criticism from fans of RE7's claustrophobic horror. The game's marketing has emphasized darkness, fog, and environmental storytelling over set-piece combat sequences.

Gameplay | Confirmed Mechanics

Resident Evil Requiem continues the over-the-shoulder third-person perspective introduced in Resident Evil 4 Remake and continued in Village's third-person mode. Capcom has confirmed there will be no first-person option, making Requiem the first mainline RE title since RE4 Remake to commit fully to third-person across the entire experience.

The inventory and crafting system has been described as a significant overhaul from Village. Capcom has shown a grid-based inventory with weight and volume constraints that force active triage decisions during play, closer in spirit to the original RE1 through RE3 box management than the more generous systems of the modern trilogy.

Enemy design emphasizes reactive behavior. Enemies respond to sound, light, and player-introduced environmental changes such as broken glass, knocked objects, and activated alarms. Capcom has described encounters as designed around avoidance and improvisation rather than combat efficiency, with ammo scarcity kept high throughout.

A co-op mode has been confirmed for specific sections of the game, described as optional and separate from the main campaign path. The implementation is closer to RE5 and RE6's co-op integration than the fully separate Shadows of Rose DLC from Village.

Platforms | PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC

Resident Evil Requiem is confirmed for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC (Steam). No last-generation versions for PS4 or Xbox One have been announced. Capcom confirmed in a financial briefing that Requiem is a current-generation-only title, allowing the team to target higher fidelity and larger world density than a cross-gen release would permit.

A PS5 Pro enhanced version has not been officially confirmed, but Capcom's recent titles including Street Fighter 6 and Dragon's Dogma 2 have received PS5 Pro patches, making a Requiem Pro enhancement likely at or shortly after launch. The RE Engine's scaling architecture is well suited to PS5 Pro's GPU upscaling capabilities.

Trailers and Reveals | Full Timeline

  • The Game Awards 2025 (December 2025): First official teaser. 90-second cinematic showing a fog-covered city street, a hooded female figure, and a brief glimpse of a deformed creature. No gameplay. Title confirmed as Resident Evil Requiem.
  • Capcom Showcase 2026 (February 2026): Extended trailer running approximately 3 minutes. First look at the city setting, partial protagonist reveal, and brief glimpses of the inventory and crafting interface. Release window confirmed as 2026.
  • State of Play June 2026: Absent. The game did not appear at Sony's June 2 State of Play, pushing the next expected reveal to summer 2026.

Latest News | June 2026

As of June 1, 2026, Capcom has not released a specific release date for Resident Evil Requiem. The game's absence from Sony's June 2 State of Play suggests a standalone Capcom announcement event or a fall showcase reveal is most likely for the final date. Insider sources have suggested the game is in final QA certification, which would be consistent with a Q4 2026 launch.

For all Resident Evil Requiem updates, bookmark this page and follow the OzoneNews Video Games hub for breaking news as announcements happen.
